title: Chalk marks
premise: Someone's private code on the brick.

stages.narration: Chalk on the corner brick — a system, clearly: arrows and slashes and a circled X, left by someone who expected to read it again. You can guess at half of it. This way clear. That way not. Here already stripped.

choices.label: Trust the marks and route by them

effects.note: routed by another scavenger's chalk, on faith

choices.label: Work the whole code out — it could be worth knowing
Scrub them out so no one tracks you by them

notes: scavenger-sign. Heeding is a small settle taken on faith (the marks could be wrong); studying spends real time for a lasting flag; scrubbing spends an hour to actually cover your trail (the safety the label promises) and settle a little.
